section_title: MITIGATION BLOCK AND OPENING RANGE GAP ENTRY
content:
The trade involves going long on a Mitigation Block.
- This Mitigation Block is identified as an up-close candle through consolidation.
- The Morning Session Opening Range Gap has been annotated.
- A Fair Value Gap (FVG) is present.
- The primary draw on liquidity is the New Week Opening Gap.
- Initial positive feedback shows price returning back into that Fair Value Gap.

section_title: MARKET CONTEXT AND STOP LOSS RATIONALE
content:
On a non-Farm Payroll Thursday, the market often exhibits a lot of 'give and take,' meaning precision
elements are not as pristine.
- The stop loss placement is trusted due to the presence of a Buy-Side Imbalance/Inefficiency.
- The expectation is to see that Buy-Side Imbalance/Inefficiency 'left open' (unmitigated).

The trade is looking for large green candles to the upside.
- The first target is a Swing Projection Target.
- Five contracts are taken out at this first target.
- The trade is utilizing the maximum funded 15-contract allowance for an $850,000 account.
- The demonstration showcases managing both initial drawdown and generating raw profit.

section_title: PROFIT TAKING AND FINAL TRADE MANAGEMENT
content:
The first partial profit of five contracts is secured.
- The stop loss is subsequently rolled to better than even.
- The limit order is adjusted to target the top end of the New Week Opening Gap.
- Further partials are taken as price enters the New Week Opening Gap.
- One contract is remaining to run to the ultimate limit order.
- The final results, including initial drawdown and subsequent profit, are shown.
